title: One question, many answers
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The code works. It writes size === four times, and uses five lines to check one number.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint2.no-switch: When the same thing is compared against several values, there is a better-suited construction.
hint2.no-break: Without it execution does not stop and carries on into the next case.
hint3.no-break: This is the most common mistake with switch. A forgotten break means the medium size also gets the large volume.
hint2.no-default: It is the case when nothing matches. Without it ml stays whatever it was.
why.if-left: There is still an if left.
hint2.if-left: Both places get rewritten. The second one wants something other than switch.
hint3.if-left: A two-outcome check that only assigns a value is written on one line with a question mark and a colon.
hint2.wrong-ml: Medium is 250 millilitres. If you get 400, a break is missing somewhere.
why.wrong-count: There are not two lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Two checks. Cup size in millilitres, and a word in singular or plural.
blocks.1.text: The code works. The first check writes size === four times in a row. The second takes five lines to choose between two words.
blocks.3.text: When the same thing is compared against several values, it can be written shorter. You say what you are comparing once, then list the cases.
blocks.5.text: The comparison is strict, like three signs. The text "3" will not match the case for the number 3.
blocks.7.text: Without it execution does not stop at its own case. It carries on down through the following ones, as if they had matched too.
blocks.8.text: Medium gets 250, then immediately 400, and 400 is what comes out. No error, no warning.
blocks.9.quote: A forgotten break is the most common mistake with switch, and it does not show until you compare the numbers.
blocks.11.text: switch compares one thing against several exact values. If the conditions are different in kind — one about size, another about distance — switch does not help.
blocks.12.text: Ranges also stay with if. You cannot write a case for over twenty-five.
blocks.14.text: A two-outcome check that only picks a value is written on one line.
blocks.16.text: The question, then a question mark, then the value for yes, a colon, and the value for no. It reads like a sentence and lets you use const instead of let.
blocks.18.text: Rewrite the first check with switch, and the second with a ternary operator.
blocks.19.text: Test by changing the size to large and the cups to 1. Then it should give 400 and cup.
